title: First Self-Described Anarchist
question: A student claims: 'William Godwin was the first political philosopher to formally identify himself as an anarchist.' Based on the passage, is this claim correct or incorrect?
input: The first political philosopher to call himself an anarchist () was Pierre-Joseph Proudhon (1809–1865), marking the formal birth of anarchism in the mid-19th century. Since the 1890s and beginning in France, libertarianism has often been used as a synonym for anarchism and its use as a synonym is still common outside the United States. Some usages of libertarianism refer to individualistic free-market philosophy only, and free-market anarchism in particular is termed libertarian anarchism.

must_identify: Pierre-Joseph Proudhon was the first to call himself an anarchist
William Godwin contributed to anarchist doctrines but did not use the term anarchist to describe himself
The formal birth of anarchism is marked in the mid-19th century by Proudhon
